rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WING SUN & CHING LAN LEE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Mo. To Reduce Settlement To Judgment
rulings.Rulings: The Court agrees with moving party that it would be improper for the Court to consider statements regarding mediation, and the Court must overturn an order which was influenced by violation of mediation confidentiality as mediation discussions cannot be used as a basis to set aside a written mediation agreement unless there is an express written waiver by all the participants to waive their confidentiality, and flagrant violation of mediation confidentiality is a serious abuse. However, the Court had not and did not consider statements regarding mediation. At oral arguments on the Motion for Judgment, the Court specifically sustained Mr. Launay's objections to Ms. Field's mediation statements and granted his Motion to strike all mediation statements. Moreover, the Court expressly stated that it did not and would not consider those statements in its decision. The moving party for reconsideration has the burden to demonstrate the motion is based on new or different law or facts and that the moving party could not, with reasonable diligence, have discovered and produce the new or different matter at an earlier stage in the proceedings. See CCP §1008(a); Baldwin v. Home Sav. of Am. (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 1192, 1198-1199; Garcia v. Hejmadi (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 674, 687-690; Forrest v. State of California Department of Corporations (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 183, 202. Here, moving Party did not present any different law or facts that the moving party could not, with reasonable diligence have discovered at an earlier proceeding. Moving party failed to meet the burden and requirement of CCP 1008(a). The Motion for Reconsideration is hereby tentatively denied. Any party who wishes to argue the motion must notify the court (415-551-3702) and opposing counsel no later than 5:00 p.m. on May 14, 2013. If the tentative ruling is not argued, it will become the order of the court. Prevailing party to prepare the order.
